The entry also covers multiple distinct works by Casanova (Geschichte meines Lebens, Eduard und Elisabeth, Vermischte Schriften, Gesammelte Briefe, and a picture volume), making the main title assignment to 'Geschichte meines Lebens' a simplification. Tanzai und Neadarne oder der Schaumlöffel. Eine japanische Geschichte. A.d.Französ. Berlin Verlag Die Schmiede XXIII, 374 S. Klassiker der erotischen Literatur. OLn. Sehr gut erhalten. .-
info.notes: missing_person: Translator is not named despite this being a translation from French. api_concerned: The publication year is missing. The Verlag Die Schmiede was active in Berlin in the 1920s; this edition likely dates from that period but cannot be confirmed without further information. The 'XXIII' before the page count likely refers to a Roman-numeral preface pagination (23 pages of front matter), not a year. Author's full name is Claude Prosper Jolyot de Crébillon (Crébillon fils); the entry uses the informal designation 'der Jüngere'.

info.notes: corrected_by_api: 'Obrosch.' expanded as Originalbroschur (standard abbreviation). api_concerned: The author's first name 'Rene' likely should be 'René' (with accent), but left uncorrected as the entry omits the accent and it is unclear if this is intentional. Also, Klaus Mann is noted as contributor of an essay but not listed in contributors field — added as contributor. Additionally, René Crevel was a French Surrealist author; 'Babylon' (Babylone, 1927) is a known work, consistent with this entry.

EISLER, Lou
Übersetzerin, Publizistin 1906-1998. Gattin von Hanns Eisler, nach dessen Tod Gattin von Ernst Fischer.
42 zeiliger hs. Brief an Charlotte Eisler vom 5.12.1942 aus Los Angeles in Englisch.
70 zeiliger hs. Brief an Charlotte Eisler vom 1.12.1945. Ein Teil davon an den Maler Georg Eisler, Sohn von Charlotte und Hanns.
3 seitiger hs. Brief an Charlotte und Georg Eisler vom 10. Dezember aus Los Angeles in Englisch
31 zeiliger hs. Brief an Charlotte und Georg Eisler vom 14. Jänner 1946
36 zeiliger hs. Brief an Charlotte Eisler aus NewYork in Englisch von 19.Dez.
2 seitiger ms. Brief an Charlotte Eisler vom 26.3.1939 aus New York mit 2 zeiliger hs. Bemerkung.
41 zeiliger ms. Brief an Charlotte Eisler vom 27. Juni 1939 aus Mexico mit hs. Korrekturen
25 zeiliger ms. Brief an Georg Eisler aus Kalifornien ohne Datum mit hs. Korrekturen.
info.notes: This entry describes a collection of 9 autograph letters (handwritten and typewritten) by Lou Eisler, addressed to Charlotte Eisler and/or Georg Eisler, written between 1939 and 1946 from various locations (Los Angeles, New York, Mexico, California). The entry includes biographical context: Lou Eisler (1906–1998), translator and publicist, was the wife of Hanns Eisler and later of Ernst Fischer. The entry is an autograph/manuscript item rather than a conventional bibliographic record; standard bibliographic fields such as publisher, year, and pages are not applicable. api_concerned is set because 'Lou Eisler' as wife of Hanns Eisler is not a widely documented figure under that name — she may be better known as 'Louise Eisler' or under another name variant; human verification of the identity is recommended.

Gutsbesitzerin 1889-1945. 45 zeiliger hs. Brief Gattendorf 11. März 1930.
info.notes: Author name 'ESTERHAZY' corrected to 'ESTERHÁZY' (missing diacritics on a well-known Hungarian noble family name). The entry describes a handwritten (hs.) letter of 45 lines, dated 11 March 1930, from Gattendorf. The dates 1889-1945 appear to be the author's life dates.

info.original_entry:
FRANCK, Martine
Fotografin, Mitglied von Magnum 1938-2012. Hs. Brief an Alice Eisler, 8 Zeilen aus Paris 07/02 auf einem Foto der Künstlerin.
Ansichtskarte mit hs. Adresse und 10 zeiligem Text der Fotografin an Alice Eisler vom 6/12/17.
info.notes: multiple_editions: The entry describes two distinct autograph items — (1) a handwritten letter of 8 lines from Paris, dated 07/02, on a photograph of the artist, addressed to Alice Eisler; and (2) a postcard with handwritten address and 10-line text addressed to Alice Eisler, dated 6/12/17. These are treated as two separate physical objects; the first is used as the main record. api_concerned: Martine Franck (1938–2012) was indeed a Belgian-French photographer and Magnum member, consistent with the entry. However, the date '6/12/17' on the second item postdates her death in 2012, which is contradictory. This may be a cataloging error (possibly the year 2007 or another date) and requires human verification. Additionally, the dates '07/02' and '6/12/17' are ambiguous in format (day/month/year vs. month/year).

2. Ms. 14. zeilige Ansichtskarte mit hs. Unterschrift an Charlotte Eisler
info.notes: This is an autograph entry describing two manuscript items by Josef Marx (Austrian composer and music theorist, 1882–1964): (1) a 23-line typed letter with handwritten signature to Charlotte Eisler, first wife of composer Hanns Eisler, dated 18 September 1962; (2) a 14-line typed postcard with handwritten signature to Charlotte Eisler. The description 'Komponist und Musikwissenschaftler' serves as a descriptor for Marx rather than a conventional title.

info.original_entry:
FORTUNATUS
Hier ist gar kuzweilig zu lesen, was Fortunatus und nach ihm…..Ein Volksbuch aus dem Jahre 1509 neu veröffentlicht vom Verlag Müller & Kiepenheuer. Mit den Holzschnitten der Erstausgabe. Übertragen und herausgegeben von Gerhard Schneider und Erwin Arndt. Hanau/Main Müller & Kiepenheuer o.J. 246 S. OLn. mit Farbschnitt und buntem Schuber. Sehr gut erhalten.
info.notes: The subtitle field contains the full descriptive text from the entry as it appears before the bibliographic data; this is unusual but reflects the entry structure. The work is a modernized retelling/adaptation of the 1509 Volksbuch 'Fortunatus', so 'Übertragen und herausgegeben' implies both translation (from Early New High German) and editing — both Schneider and Arndt are listed as translators and editors simultaneously. Publication year is given as 'o.J.' (ohne Jahr / no year). The Farbschnitt (colored page edges) is noted in format; the Schuber (slipcase) is also captured in packaging. api_concerned: the subtitle text includes 'kuzweilig' which is likely a typo for 'kurzweilig', but since this appears to be a direct transcription of the original title page wording, no correction has been applied.

Additionally, the author's given name is not provided — only 'GRILLPARZER' appears; the full name is Franz Grillparzer. Human verification recommended.
